name: Hautacam Ridges Loop
description: Hike the easy 3.9-mile Hautacam Ridges Loop in the French Pyrenees, offering panoramic mountain views and taking about 1 hour 42 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around Beaucens is an excellent way to experience the French Pyrenees, characterized by its panoramic mountain views, majestic peaks, and serene high-altitude lakes. The region, nestled in the Hautes-Pyrénées department, offers diverse terrain from the accessible trails of Hautacam to the expansive panoramas from peaks like Pic du Pibeste. Hikers can explore valleys, mountain passes such as Col du Tramassel, and various high-altitude lakes. Best hiking trails around Beaucens The most popular hiking route is Hautacam Ridges Loop, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This easy route offers panoramic mountain views from accessible trails. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lake Isaby – Hautacam circus loop from Pic de Naouit, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail leads through the Hautacam area, featuring serene high-altitude lakes and mountain scenery. Local hikers also love the Moulata Pass – Lake Bassias loop from Col de Tramassel, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through mountain passes and lakes, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Beaucens is defined by panoramic mountain views, majestic peaks, and serene high-altitude lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging routes. The routes in Beaucens are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 390 reviews. More than 3600 hikers have used komoot to explore Beaucens's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Beaucens?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Beaucens, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find options for various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Beaucens?
A: Hiking around Beaucens offers stunning panoramic mountain views, majestic peaks, and serene high-altitude lakes. The region is characterized by the dramatic scenery of the French Pyrenees, including features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/347701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount de Gez&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Beaucens?
A: Yes, Beaucens offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877276396/hautacam-ridges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hautacam Ridges Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km long and takes about 1 hour 42 minutes to complete, offering accessible trails with panoramic mountain views. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41445415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulata Pass – Lake Isaby loop from Col de Moulata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: The trails around Beaucens are rich with natural beauty. You can discover several picturesque lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1036163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Dets Coubous&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Port Bielh&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5448339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake of Lesponne&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features prominent peaks like Pic du Pibeste, offering expansive panoramas of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in the Beaucens area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Beaucens are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877276396/hautacam-ridges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hautacam Ridges Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35414611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Isaby – Hautacam circus loop from Pic de Naouit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29025937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulata Pass – Lake Bassias loop from Col de Tramassel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Beaucens?
A: The best time for hiking in Beaucens is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically mild, and the trails are clear of snow, offering ideal conditions to enjoy the mountain scenery and high-altitude lakes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Beaucens?
A: The trails in Beaucens are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the panoramic mountain views, the serene high-altitude lakes, and the diverse terrain that caters to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, the region provides more strenuous options. While specific challenging routes are not detailed here, the Pyrenees context suggests trails that are part of long-distance treks like the GR10, involving significant elevation gains and requiring robust physical fitness and alpine experience.

Q: Is Beaucens a good base for exploring the wider Pyrenees region?
A: Absolutely. Beaucens is an ideal base for exploring the wider Pyrenees region. Its location provides access to natural wonders such as the Cirque de Gavarnie, Pont d'Espagne, and Lac de Gaube, all known for their dramatic mountain scenery, crystal-clear lakes, and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any mountain huts or refuges along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, for those undertaking longer hikes or seeking an overnight stay, there are mountain huts in the vicinity. One such refuge is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge de Campana de Cloutou&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a welcome stop on more extensive routes.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes around Beaucens?
A: Hikes around Beaucens vary significantly in duration. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877276396/hautacam-ridges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hautacam Ridges Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under two hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29025937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulata Pass – Lake Bassias loop from Col de Tramassel&lt;/a&gt;, might take around four hours, while more challenging routes can extend to five hours or more.
tips: From Hautacam you can still continue to Tramassel for an even more splendid view. A small refuge serving food and drink awaits you near the car park
The evening before the sunset is the best light

faqs.question: Where does the Hautacam Ridges Loop trail start?
faqs.answer: The Hautacam Ridges Loop typically starts from the Hautacam ski resort area. This is a convenient point for parking and accessing the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Hautacam Ridges Loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered easy and is suitable for all skill levels and any fitness level. It's a great option for beginners and families looking for a pleasant mountain walk.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect open, green mountain landscapes with a mix of unpaved, natural, and gravel paths. The route offers panoramic views of the Pyrenean mountains and the Gave de Pau valley.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking landscapes and panoramic views. You'll pass by the View of Hautacam and traverse the Col de Tramassel Plateau, both offering stunning vistas.

faqs.question: Is the Hautacam Ridges Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general Hautacam area is welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts, specific regulations for dogs on this particular loop are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Hautacam Ridges Loop. The area is generally open for public access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Hautacam Ridges Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is mild and the trails are clear of snow. During winter, conditions can be challenging due to snow and ice, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hautacam ski resort, which serves as the starting point, offers various amenities. You can usually find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities there, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Are there more challenging hiking options nearby for experienced hikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, while this loop is easy, the Hautacam region offers more challenging routes. For experienced hikers, the 'Trails du Hautacam' include physically demanding tours with significant elevation gain, often reserved for experienced hikers or trail runners.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and relatively short duration, comfortable walking shoes, water, and appropriate clothing for the weather are usually sufficient. Don't forget a camera for the panoramic views!

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the Hautacam Ridges Loop trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the Hautacam ski resort can be limited, especially outside of peak seasons. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from nearby towns like Argelès-Gazost or Lourdes, or consider driving to the trailhead.
